name: Ovarian Cancer - Diagnosis Code Query (Multi-Histology, Female-Specific)
description: **First female-specific cancer phenotype** (counterpart to male-only Prostate Cancer). Tests ability to find ovarian cancer patients via diagnosis codes by histology: parent ovarian malignancy, primary, neoplasm (broader), serous papillary cystadenocarcinoma (most common epithelial), and endometrioid carcinoma. All patients in expected results are female.
prompt: Find all female patients with a diagnosis of ovarian cancer, including primary malignancy of ovary, serous papillary cystadenocarcinoma, mucinous cystadenocarcinoma, and endometrioid carcinoma of ovary.

prompts.naive: Find all patients with ovarian cancer.
prompts.broad: Find all patients diagnosed with ovarian cancer — including the parent malignant neoplasm of ovary, primary malignant neoplasm of ovary, neoplasm of ovary (broader), serous papillary cystadenocarcinoma (most common epithelial subtype), and endometrioid carcinoma of ovary.
prompts.expert: I need to identify all ovarian cancer patients. Ovarian cancer is documented under multiple SNOMED codes from the ASCO Epithelial Ovarian Cancer VSAC: Malignant neoplasm of ovary (363443007) is the parent, Primary malignant neoplasm of ovary (93934004), Neoplasm of ovary (123843001) — broader, Serous papillary cystadenocarcinoma ovary (254850005), and Endometrioid carcinoma ovary (254852002). Search Condition resources for all five codes. Note this phenotype is female-only — male patients should not appear in expected results.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with ovarian cancer diagnosis codes. **First female-specific phenotype.** Tests histology code variation.
